key_insight: The paper integrates differentiable finite-element structural analysis into stellarator coil optimization, allowing coil geometry and support clamp locations to be optimized together for both magnetic accuracy and lower stress.
engineering_relevance: This could shorten and automate the structural design of fusion magnet systems by reducing manual FEA iteration while improving coil durability and manufacturability.
method: Built coil-fem, an open-source tool that adds differentiable support-structure FEA to the stellarator coil optimization loop.
Optimized coil shape and clamp placement simultaneously using a stress penalty alongside magnetic-field objectives.
Targeted a three-way tradeoff among field quality, structural rigidity, and geometric simplicity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a design with 2.4× lower RMS von Mises stress and similar field error compared with an unoptimized baseline.
watch_item: The result is demonstrated on an optimization study, so practical gains may depend on the accuracy, runtime, and robustness of the differentiable FEA model in full engineering workflows.
paper_title: Towards joint optimization of stellarator coils and support structures
